title: Village of Croton-on-Hudson Transitions Fleet to Renewable Diesel for Cleaner Operations
content: The Village of Croton-on-Hudson, NY, has announced the transition of its entire municipal heavy-duty fleet to Renewable Diesel (RD) following a successful three-month pilot. This cleaner fuel initiative covers all diesel-powered vehicles, such as garbage trucks and snow plows, serving as a seamless drop-in replacement that requires no mechanical modifications. The switch is projected to reduce the Villageâ€™s carbon footprint by roughly 12.5% (186 metric tons of CO2 annually) and lower nitrogen oxide emissions. Although Renewable Diesel costs approximately $1 more per gallon than petroleum dieselâ€”totaling an extra $28,000 annuallyâ€”the expense is fully covered by the Villageâ€™s sustainability fund, generated from a community solar array, ensuring no cost to taxpayers. This move reinforces Croton-on-Hudsonâ€™s status as New York Stateâ€™s top-ranked Clean Energy Community.
author: Analysts Account
